videos.title: First in 50 Years: Ben Ogden Wins Historic Silver for USA in Cross-Country Skiing | Full Story
videos.description:
🥈 HISTORIC: Ben Ogden won SILVER in cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Olympics—the first American man to medal in the sport in 50 YEARS.

The 25-year-old from Landgrove, Vermont (population 177) finished just 0.8 seconds behind Norwegian legend Johannes Klaebo, ending a half-century drought and becoming only the second U.S. man ever to medal in Olympic cross-country skiing.

Then he did a BACKFLIP off the podium. "I promised my 15-year-old self I would do that."

This is the story of Ben Ogden, Bill Koch, Vermont ski culture, and why this moment changes everything for American cross-country skiing.

📍 KEY DETAILS:
- Ben Ogden wins SILVER in men's sprint classic (3:40.61)
- First U.S. men's medal in cross-country skiing since Bill Koch in 1976
- Only second American man EVER to medal in the sport
- From Landgrove, VT (pop. 177) - studied mechanical engineering at UVM
- Finished 0.8 seconds behind Johannes Klaebo's gold (7th Olympic gold for Klaebo)
- Grew up skiing in Bill Koch Youth Ski League - trained on Koch's backyard trails
- Father John Ogden (former Middlebury skier) died in 2023 - didn't see son's medal
- Did backflip off podium fulfilling promise to 15-year-old self
- Team USA has now won 4 cross-country medals since 2018 (after 1 in previous 94 years)

🔍 WHAT WE COVER:
- The race breakdown: How Ogden won silver in the sprint classic
- The 50-year drought: Why this is so historic for Team USA
- Who is Ben Ogden? Vermont roots, engineering degree, car restoration hobby
- The Vermont pipeline: Bill Koch → Jessie Diggins → Ben Ogden
- The competition: Beating the world's best (except Klaebo)
- What this means for American cross-country skiing's future
- The backflip moment and why he did it

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 - Opening: First in 50 Years
0:45 - Subscribe for Olympic Stories
1:00 - The Race: How Ogden Won Silver
3:00 - The Drought: 50 Years Without a Medal
5:00 - Who is Ben Ogden? Vermont Engineer Turned Olympian
7:00 - The Vermont Pipeline: Building Olympic Champions
8:30 - The Competition: Beating the World's Best
10:00 - What This Means for America
11:30 - Closing: The Backflip Promise

📰 SOURCES:
- NBC Olympics, NPR, CBS News
- U.S. Ski & Snowboard Team
- Olympics.com interviews
- NCAA coverage
- Boston Globe, Yahoo Sports

👍 LIKE if you love underdog Olympic stories
💬 COMMENT: Did this inspire you? Will this spark a new era for U.S. cross-country?
🔔 SUBSCRIBE for more Olympic coverage beyond the highlights

#BenOgden #Olympics2026 #CrossCountrySkiing #TeamUSA #Silver #Vermont #WinterOlympics #Historic #BillKoch #MilanCortina2026 #Olympic #Skiing #Underdog #Inspiration

⚖️ DISCLAIMER: This video celebrates athletic achievement and tells the human story behind the medal.

videos.title: Wolfdog jumps into Olympic XC ski race, goes for gold | Winter Olympics 2026 | NBC Sports
videos.description:
In the women’s cross-country skiing team sprint qualification, a dog ran onto the course and zoomed with two skiers across the finish line. The good boy's sprint was caught by the photo finish camera. #NBCSports #WinterOlympics #CrossCountrySkiing

videos.title: Owen Wilson and the American Birkebeiner cross-country ski race | Here Comes the Sun
videos.description:
Actor Owen Wilson sits down with Ben Mankiewicz to discuss his latest film, “Paint.” Then, Conor Knighton takes a trip to Wisconsin to learn about the American Birkebeiner. “Here Comes the Sun” is a closer look at some of the people, places and things we bring you every week on “CBS Sunday Morning.”

#news #celebrity #herecomesthesun 

CBS News Streaming Network is the premier 24/7 anchored streaming news service from CBS News and Stations, available free to everyone with access to the Internet. The CBS News Streaming Network is your destination for breaking news, live events and original reporting locally, nationally and around the globe. Launched in November 2014 as CBSN, the CBS News Streaming Network is available live in 91 countries and on 30 digital platforms and apps, as well as on CBSNews.com and Paramount+.

up next an American event inspired by
Norwegian history
welcome back each year thousands of
athletes and enthusiasts descend on a

largest and as Connor Knighton shows us
what just might be the most entertaining

each winter thousands of cross-country
skiers from all across the world travel
to the North Woods of Wisconsin to
travel more than 30 miles between the
towns of cable and Hayward
the American burkabiner is the largest
and the enthusiasm here is contagious we

Berkey for short could fly by in a

skiers the leading up to the main event
there are days of activities there are
shorter races for children
foreign
lovers ski with their pets teams of six
compete in giant ski races it's kind of
like Comic-Con for athletes is how I'm
looking at it Jackie and dandaroma are
Avid skiers Jackie has skied the Berkey
four times Dan 17 but for this year's
race they wanted to do things a little
differently these other Racers are going
to be in spandex Gore-Tex you know what
are you gonna be wearing full on fur
Jackie Dan and Dan's Father Jim were
selected to be mascots of sorts skiing
the entire course on wooden skis dressed
as Norwegian folk Heroes all while
carrying a baby doll it's a tradition
with roots and a far older history
as portrayed in the film The Last King
in the early 13th century Norway was in
the middle of a civil war after King
Hogan III died members of the
birkavainer faction skied his son the
heir to the throne through the woods to
safety
cheer in Wisconsin three skiers reenact
the rescue I think the the fact that
there is this real story about an event
that now you're recreating it I think
that's pretty darn cool and pretty
special and I think it resonates with a
lot of people Ben pop is the executive
director of the American burkabiner
which is modeled after a Norwegian race
there every competitor skis with a baby
weight backpack to honor the Warriors
journey of course you may be asking
yourself why is this crazy Norwegian
history have anything to do with Hayward
Wisconsin in the middle of Northwestern
Wisconsin nowhere it all came down to
one man Tony wise Tony wise was born in
Hayward and was a born marketer after
serving in World War II he decided to
bring the competitive skiing he observed
in Europe back home to Wisconsin a place
where a Scandinavian ancestry is common
as a way to encourage tourism his first
Berkey race held 50 years ago had just
35 participants most of whom had never
crossed country skied before
I swore up and down after that first I

but
Jermaine is the only competitor who's

challenging that people just myself
included love that aspect of it
the race itself has faced challenges
posed by warmer Winters in recent
decades it's been canceled twice due to
lack of snow it's really important that
we look at climate change and weather
because if we don't have it well it's a
big loss the event provides a major
boost for the local economy this town of
fewer than 3 000 residents hosts close
to 40 000 Spectators on race day
hayward's Main Street overflows with

the church bells signal the arrival of

women's division
but the biggest cheers of the day were

they skied onto Main Street and swapped

videos.title: Cross Country Skiing Beijing 2022 | Men's 15km Classic highlights
videos.description:
📲 Subscribe to @olympics: http://oly.ch/Subscribe 

 

Finland's Iivo Niskanen claimed gold in the men's cross country skiing 15km classic, his second medal of Beijing 2022 after his bronze in the 30km skiathlon. His winning time of 37:54.8 was an impressive 23 seconds ahead of ROC's Alexander Bolshunov who took silver. Bronze went to Johannes Klaebo of Norway
